tossups.question: In a 2017 biography of this man, Michael Jones discredits an account of this man’s slaughter of three thousand civilians during a siege. This man led two devastating raids called chevauchées (“shuh-voh-SHAY”) into territory held by Jean I, Count d’Armagnac. This man helped Peter of Castile to defeat his half-brother Henry of Trastamara at the Battle of Nájera (“NAH-hay-rah”). The franc was created after this man posted a ransom of three million écus (“ay-KOO”) for the release of a captured king in the Treaty of Brétigny (“bray-teen-YEE”). This commander adopted a crest of three ostrich feathers from the helmet of John I of Bohemia, who was killed in the Battle of Crécy (“cray-SEE”). This son of Edward III took John II of France prisoner while commanding troops at the Battle of Poitiers (“pwaht-YAY”). For 10 points, name this first Prince of Wales, whose nickname might come from his dark armor.

packet.name: H (Cambridge B, Berkeley B, Columbia B, and Cornell)

tossups.answer_sanitized: Edward the Black Prince [accept Edward of Woodstock; prompt on Edward]
tossups.question_sanitized: In a 2017 biography of this man, Michael Jones discredits an account of this man's slaughter of three thousand civilians during a siege. This man led two devastating raids called chevauchees ("shuh-voh-SHAY") into territory held by Jean I, Count d'Armagnac. This man helped Peter of Castile to defeat his half-brother Henry of Trastamara at the Battle of Najera ("NAH-hay-rah"). The franc was created after this man posted a ransom of three million ecus ("ay-KOO") for the release of a captured king in the Treaty of Bretigny ("bray-teen-YEE"). This commander adopted a crest of three ostrich feathers from the helmet of John I of Bohemia, who was killed in the Battle of Crecy ("cray-SEE"). This son of Edward III took John II of France prisoner while commanding troops at the Battle of Poitiers ("pwaht-YAY"). For 10 points, name this first Prince of Wales, whose nickname might come from his dark armor.

tossups.question: This man’s creation as Earl of Chester and Duke of Cornwall marked the first creation of an English duke. He won the Battle of Nájera after intervening to support Pedro of Castile. He practice chevauchée, the practice of pillaging towns, and massacred the inhabitants of Limoges and Caen. This consort of Joan of Kent became the first member of the Order of the Garter, and he was the first Prince of Wales not to be crowned king. For 10 points, name this prince who defeated the French at Crécy and Poitiers and was the eldest son of Edward III.
tossups.answer: Edward of Woodstock [or Edward the Black Prince; prompt on “Edward”]
tossups.answer_sanitized: Edward of Woodstock [or Edward the Black Prince; prompt on "Edward"]
tossups.question_sanitized: This man's creation as Earl of Chester and Duke of Cornwall marked the first creation of an English duke. He won the Battle of Najera after intervening to support Pedro of Castile. He practice chevauchee, the practice of pillaging towns, and massacred the inhabitants of Limoges and Caen. This consort of Joan of Kent became the first member of the Order of the Garter, and he was the first Prince of Wales not to be crowned king. For 10 points, name this prince who defeated the French at Crecy and Poitiers and was the eldest son of Edward III.

tossups.question: This man is the subject of a historical poem by the Chandos Herald. This man and his younger brother won the Battle of Najera in support of Peter the Cruel in the Castilian Civil War. A revolt against this man resulted in him sacking Limoges. One legend states that this man gained his crest and motto after the death of John of Bohemia in one battle. Jean de Clermont died at a battle won by Jean III de Grailly and this man. He captured the French king John II at that battle, after which he served as the first and only Prince of Aquitaine. Earlier, this man helped his father win the Battle of Crecy. For 10 points, name this son of Edward III who won the Battle of Poitiers and was possibly nicknamed for the color of his armor.
tossups.answer: Edward the Black Prince [or Edward of Woodstock; prompt on Edward]
tossups.answer_sanitized: Edward the Black Prince [or Edward of Woodstock; prompt on Edward]

He captured the French king John II at that battle, after which he served as the first and only Prince of Aquitaine. Earlier, this man helped his father win the Battle of Crecy. For 10 points, name this son of Edward III who won the Battle of Poitiers and was possibly nicknamed for the color of his armor.

bonuses.leadin: A failed “Great” one of these campaigns in 1373 led by John of Gaunt saw English forces lose 15,000 people while moving from Calais to Gascony. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this type of short raiding campaign, commonly launched by the English cavalry in the Hundred Years’ War. This kind of raid focused on pillaging farms and industry while avoiding major cities.

This English prince and eldest son of King Edward III led many chevauchées in the 1350s, including one that ended in the Battle of Poitiers and the capture of John II. He was also the brother of John of Gaunt.
In a later campaign, the Black Prince defeated Henry of Trastámara at this 1367 battle named for a small Spanish town, fighting on behalf of Peter the Cruel during the Civil War of Castile.
answers: Edward the Black Prince [or Edward of Woodstock; prompt on Edward]
answers_sanitized: Edward the Black Prince [or Edward of Woodstock; prompt on Edward]

This English prince and eldest son of King Edward III led many chevauchees in the 1350s, including one that ended in the Battle of Poitiers and the capture of John II. He was also the brother of John of Gaunt.
In a later campaign, the Black Prince defeated Henry of Trastamara at this 1367 battle named for a small Spanish town, fighting on behalf of Peter the Cruel during the Civil War of Castile.

bonuses.leadin: Michael Jones argues that this man was not responsible for the sack of Limoges (“lee-MUZH”), positing that the French carried out the massacre as punishment for letting this man into the city. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this English soldier and the first Prince of Wales, nicknamed “the Black Prince.”

Edward the Black Prince legendarily knighted John Hawkwood, one of these Italian mercenaries. These soldiers contracted out their private armies to various city-states vying for control in 14th-century Italy.

Edward the Black Prince fought at the Battle of Nájera (“NAH-hay-rah”) during a civil war in this kingdom. Another celebrated mercenary, Bertrand du Guesclin (“bair-TRAWN due gay-CLAN”), or the “Black Dog of Brocéliande,” fought in that civil war in this kingdom.
answers: Edward the Black Prince [accept Edward Of Woodstock, Prince d’Aquitaine, Prince of Wales, Duke of Cornwall, Earl of Chester]

packet.name: B. Florida B, Princeton B, UNC A

answers_sanitized: Edward the Black Prince [accept Edward Of Woodstock, Prince d'Aquitaine, Prince of Wales, Duke of Cornwall, Earl of Chester]

bonuses.leadin: The text of this novel ends with a number of postscripts by the characters, who supposedly read the main text and then offered their own versions of the story. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this novel in which Bradley Pearson is convicted for the murder of Arnold Baffin after falling in love with Baffin’s daughter Julian.
This other novel is narrated by the arrogant, retired playwright Charles Arrowby, as he recalls his past loves and encounters some possibly supernatural monsters in the title location near his house.
The Black Prince and The Sea, The Sea are by this novelist and philosopher, who wrote Under the Net.

bonuses.leadin: During this event, nobles besieged in the citadel of Meaux were relieved by forces under Gaston Phoebus, and the city was burned down in retribution. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this French peasants' revolt led by Guillaume Cale and supported by Etienne Marcel, which threw France into chaos in the middle of the Hundred Years' War.

This nobleman tricked Guillaume Cale into a supposed parley at Mello, then had him beheaded. This treacherous king of Navarre was jailed by John II for trying to help the Dauphin overthrow him, and later turned against the Dauphin to get his hands on Normandy.
The Jacquerie took place after John II was captured at the Battle of Poitiers, whose victor was this English prince nicknamed for the color of his armor.
answers: Charles the Bad [or Charles II of Navarre; prompt on Charles]
Edward the Black Prince [or Edward of Woodstock; prompt on Edward]
answers_sanitized: Charles the Bad [or Charles II of Navarre; prompt on Charles]
Edward the Black Prince [or Edward of Woodstock; prompt on Edward]

bonuses.leadin: Political squabbling between the victorious commanders at this battle gave an opening for the defeated pretender, Henry of Trastamara, to assassinate his brother at Montiel two years later. For 10 points each:
parts: Identify this 1367 clash during the Castilian Civil War, won by Pedro the Cruel thanks to English support led by a pair of brothers including John of Gaunt.

This man was that brother of John, who in the wake of Najera led his troops on a disastrous, dysentery-plagued retreat through France. His prolific military career included winning the naval battle of Winchelsea, massacring the populace of Limoges, and capturing John the Good and Philip the Bold in 1356.

Henry was able to foment unrest against Pedro by using the latter's support of this faith, which Henry began slaughtering wholesale during the war. Things again got dicey for this group in Spain after the Alhambra decree, which led many to become “Marranos,” practicing their faith in secret.
answers: Edward, the Black Prince [or Edward of Woodstock; prompt on partial answer]
answers_sanitized: Edward, the Black Prince [or Edward of Woodstock; prompt on partial answer]
